title: Medical Ethics in Neonatal Care
authors: Haward MF, Fanaroff JM, Laventhal NT

blocks.text:
ทำไม neonatologist ต้องเข้าใจ medical ethics?
NICU คือ environment ที่ ethical discourse มีความเข้มข้นที่สุดในทางการแพทย์ — every day decisions affect life, QoL, and family values. (1) 4 Beauchamp & Childress principles: autonomy, beneficence, nonmaleficence, justice; (2) Moral dilemma ≠ moral distress; (3) Gray zone 22-25 wks: NIH Outcomes Estimator + parental values; (4) Withholding = Withdrawing ทาง ethics; (5) brain death: term >=37 wks — 2 exams >=24 h — ไม่มี criteria สำหรับ preterm.
เป้าหมายการเรียนรู้วันนี้
ท่อง 4 หลักการของ Beauchamp & Childress และยกตัวอย่าง NICU scenario สำหรับแต่ละข้อ
แยกความต่างระหว่าง moral dilemma, moral distress, และ moral residue
อธิบาย parental authority vs parental autonomy; harm principle (Diekema) vs best interests standard
เปรียบเทียบ shared decision making models: informative, deliberative-interactive, assent, negotiated
ระบุ 3 approaches ต่อ prognostic uncertainty: threshold, wait-until-certainty, trial of therapy
อธิบาย antenatal consultation at margin of viability: 3 aims, NIH Outcomes Estimator 5 factors
ระบุ 3 criteria W&W + 10-step end-of-life framework
Brain death: AAP criteria, term vs preterm, DCD protocol
อธิบาย double effect; ทำไม NMB ห้ามใช้; Groningen protocol
HHS research consent categories: กี่ parent + risk level
1. Four Principles of Medical Ethics (Beauchamp & Childress 2019)
1.1 Autonomy — Parental Authority
Parental authority (not autonomy) — คำที่ถูกต้อง; autonomy = for oneself only. 2 ฐาน: (1) presumption parents act in child's best interests; (2) mechanism of shared decision making
"We want everything done": ต้อง explore เสมอ — อาจหมายถึง fear of abandonment
Harm principle (Diekema DS. Theor Med Bioeth 2004;25:243): harm threshold คือจุดที่ override parental decision ได้; ดีกว่า "best interests" เมื่อ uncertainty สูง
1.2 Beneficence — Best Interests of the Newborn
Best interests: NDD outcome, motor/vision/hearing, pain/suffering, technology dependence, capacity for meaningful interaction
Meadow et al. Pediatrics 2008;121:732: ผู้เชี่ยวชาญทำนายว่า infant จะไม่ survive to discharge — ผิดบ่อย — ควรระวังการ prognosticate
ระวัง judging quality of life of others — คนพิการอาจ lead fulfilling meaningful life
1.3 Nonmaleficence + Justice
Nonmaleficence: primum non nocere; harm = pain, disability, death, futile interventions; historical lesson: neonatal surgeries without anesthesia
Justice: macroallocation vs microallocation; racial disparities in NICU (Sigurdson et al. Pediatrics 2019); COVID: autonomy may yield to utilitarian/justice when resources scarce
2. Moral Dilemma, Distress, and Decision Making
2.1 Moral Dilemma vs Moral Distress vs Moral Residue

rows: Parents want aggressive care; team believes comfort care is in best interest
Know right thing; CONSTRAINED by lack of authority/institutional pressure
Nurse sees futile treatment; feels powerless to act
Repeated unresolved cases → moral injury in NICU staff

blocks.text: Moral resilience (Rushton et al. Am J Nurs 2016;116:40): constructive approach — direct distress toward positive actions for patient care improvement
2.2 Shared Decision Making Models
Informative (common in NICU): provide information; parents decide independently — ignores emotional/heuristic processes
Deliberative-interactive (ideal): clinician helps clarify values AND makes own reasoning transparent — uncommonly achieved
Negotiated model (Leuthner — most ethically appropriate): parental input reflects both physician + parent values
Personalized decision making: variability in risk tolerance, decisional responsibility, affective/cognitive needs
2.3 Communication Guidelines (Box 4.2)

Communication Guidelines + ouRHOPE Mnemonic
Do: truthful, pre-emptive, acknowledge uncertainty, understandable language, time to process, disclose errors. Avoid: jargon, info overload, impersonal labels ("the hypoplast"). McDonagh et al. Crit Care Med 2004;32:1484: family satisfaction correlates with FREQUENCY of family speech. ouRHOPE (Racine et al. 2017): Reflection, Humility, Open-mindedness, Partnership, Engagement — for neurologic injury consultations.
3. Gestational Viability — The Gray Zone
3.1 Current Thresholds + NIH Outcomes Estimator
Viability border: 23 → 22 weeks (Backes et al. 2021); some neurologically intact survivors; >=24 weeks: resuscitate absent major co-morbidities
Self-fulfilling prophecy: no care at GA X → no survivors → "proves" poor prognosis

Tyson JE et al. NEJM 2008;358:1672 — NIH Outcomes Estimator: 5 Factors
(1) GA, (2) EBW, (3) singleton, (4) antenatal steroids, (5) sex. Free online at NICHD. "Less arbitrary, more individualized" than GA thresholds alone. ใช้ร่วมกับ parental values เสมอ.
3.2 Approaches to Prognostic Uncertainty

rows: Self-fulfilling prophecy; cannot accept forgoing outlier survival
ICU for all with any chance; continue until unequivocal adverse findings
False promise of certainty; parents as bystanders
Individualize: epi + individual factors + parental values; moral responsibility guides continuation

blocks.text:
Fig. 4.2 — Kukora & Boss: Physician vs Parent "Good Outcome" Definitions
Physician (A): high nonsurvival + impairment = dismal; comfort care logical. Parent (B): ANY survival = good; dying after trying = acceptable. Values differ — not misunderstanding. 3 antenatal consultation aims: (1) develop relationship + explore values; (2) individualized information; (3) support decision-making about trial of therapy.
4. Withholding and Withdrawing Life-Sustaining Medical Treatment
4.1 Moral Equivalence + Language

Withholding = Withdrawing ทาง Ethics — Language = "Redirection of Care"
"If morally right/wrong to withhold — equally right/wrong to withdraw." Wall & Partridge Pediatrics 1997. ห้ามใช้: "withdraw support/care" — ทำให้ families กลัว abandonment. ใช้: "withdrawal of life-sustaining medical treatment" / "redirection of care". บอก parents: "เรายังดูแลลูกคุณต่อไป — เปลี่ยน goals จาก curative เป็น comfort"
4.2 3 Criteria + 10-Step End-of-Life Framework
(1) Inevitability of death: likely to die regardless; extra days may be valued by family; prediction difficult (Meadow 2008)
(2) Ineffective treatment: not met goals; imparts pain/suffering without appreciable benefit
(3) Poor quality of life: limited cognition/mobility or continued pain — valid and ethically permissible
Use "potentially inappropriate treatment" not "futility" (Kon & Davidson Crit Care Nurse 2017)
10-step framework (Feudtner Pediatr Clin North Am 2007):
1. Prepare: team debrief; unified prognosis; ouRHOPE
2. Optimal environment: private, quiet, away from bedside; primary nurse included
3. Address emotions: "I am concerned" / "I wish" statements first
4. Identify ethical problems: disentangle from communication issues; ethics consult if needed
5. Rightful decision makers: parents by default; document thoroughly
6. Relevant facts: "Good ethics begins with good facts" — medical + cultural/religious
7. Explore options: risks, benefits, alternatives; no coercion
8. Develop consensus: unilateral W only after ALL avenues exhausted including transfer for 2nd opinion
9. Implement: manner of death; religious rituals; opioids for comfort; NO NMB
10. Follow-up: validate parents; team debrief — prevent moral residue/burnout
5. Brain Death, DCD, and Organ Donation

AAP Brain Death — Term Neonates >=37+0 Weeks ONLY
Nakagawa et al. Pediatrics 2011;128:e720 (reaffirmed 2015): >=37+0 wks GA; known cause of irreversible coma; 2 exams >=24 h apart (>=12 h if >30 days old). NO criteria for preterm infants (developmental immaturity). Anencephaly ≠ brain death (brainstem function retained). Apnea test: bradycardia risk — consult hospital policy first.
DCD (Canadian guideline): 5-minute hands-off + arterial catheter to confirm arrest. Successful neonatal DCD: heart, kidney, hepatocytes. Conflict of interest rule: care team ≠ organ recovery team
6. Analgesic Agents — Double Effect

Double Effect — Opioids ✓ | NMB ✗ | Active Euthanasia ✗
Partridge JC, Wall SN. Pediatrics 1997;99:76: opioids in most W&W cases — double effect. Intent = relieve suffering = ethically appropriate. NMB: intent = ensure death → INAPPROPRIATE (Truog et al. NEJM 2000;342:508). Groningen protocol (Netherlands): deliberate ending of life in intractable suffering — most authorities: unsupportable. Rule: dose titrated to comfort ≠ dose to hasten death.
7. Ethics of Research in the NICU
Therapeutic misconception: erroneous belief research = therapeutic; explain randomization clearly
Clinical equipoise (Freedman NEJM 1987): genuine uncertainty = moral requirement for RCT; consent = continuous process
8. Ethical Responsibilities of Neonatal Physicians

Box 4.3 — Ethical Responsibilities Summary
To all: just, equitable, inclusive; address unconscious bias. To patients: "right and good" in best interests. To parents: constructive, respectful relationship. To team: leadership; open questioning culture. To trainees: educational experience; model of professionalism. To self: moral conscience and agency.
9. NICU Practical
"We want everything done": explore meaning first — may be fear of abandonment
Gray zone consult: NIH Outcomes Estimator (5 factors); trial of therapy; explore parental values (Fig 4.2); avoid "nonviable fetus" if parents have named baby
Before family meeting: brief team; unified message; invite primary nurse; parents choose attendees
At withdrawal: manner of death; religious rituals; opioids titrated to comfort; NO NMB; "redirection of care" language
Brain death: term >=37 wks only; 2 exams >=24 h; consult policy; preterm = cannot diagnose
Research: separate recruitment from clinical care; one study at a time; continuous consent
Team wellbeing: debrief after difficult cases; moral residue → burnout if unresolved
10. 5 Bedside Pearls

PEARL 1 — 4 Principles = Common Language — ใช้แต่ไม่พอ
Beauchamp & Childress 4 principles ช่วย articulate ethical questions และ unite struggling teams. เมื่อหลักการขัดแย้งกัน (beneficence vs autonomy) — principalism ไม่ช่วย; ต้องใช้ ethics committee + personalized discussion. Ethical reasoning = nuanced reflection + moral agency, ไม่ใช่ checklist.

PEARL 2 — Moral Distress ≠ Moral Dilemma — แยกให้ออก แล้วค่อยแก้
Moral dilemma: shared decision making + ethics consultation. Moral distress: constrained — debrief + psychological safety + proactive communication. Moral residue + crescendo: ถ้าไม่จัดการ → burnout. NICU leadership ต้องสร้าง culture ที่ safe to speak up.

PEARL 3 — Gray Zone: NIH Tool + Parental Values — ไม่ใช้ GA อย่างเดียว
Tyson NEJM 2008 — NIH Outcomes Estimator (5 factors). Fig 4.2 (Kukora & Boss): parent อาจ define "good outcome" ต่างจาก physician (any survival vs intact survival). Self-fulfilling prophecy warning. Trial of therapy + moral responsibility = recommended approach.

PEARL 4 — Withholding = Withdrawing; Double Effect: Opioids ✓ | NMB ✗
W&W ทาง ethics: identical. Language: "redirection of care". Opioids: double effect = ethically appropriate. NMB: intent to ensure death = ethically inappropriate (Truog NEJM 2000). Groningen = controversial; most authorities: unsupportable.

PEARL 5 — Brain Death: Term >=37 wks, 2 Exams >=24 h — Preterm = ไม่มี Criteria
AAP 2011 (reaffirmed 2015): >=37+0 wks GA; 2 exams >=24 h apart. Preterm: no criteria (developmental immaturity). Anencephaly ≠ brain death. Apnea test: bradycardia risk — consult hospital policy. Once declared: no parental permission needed to discontinue support.
11. Evidence Update — Key References
Beauchamp TL, Childress J. Principles of Biomedical Ethics. 8th ed. Oxford; 2019: 4 core principles; foundational bioethics reference worldwide.
Tyson JE et al. NEJM 2008;358:1672: NIH Outcomes Estimator — 5 factors; individualized prognosis beyond gestational age.
Meadow W et al. Pediatrics 2008;121:732: Serial death predictions — frequently incorrect; caution with prognostication.
Diekema DS. Theor Med Bioeth 2004;25:243: Harm principle — threshold for overriding parental refusal.
Kukora SK, Boss RD. Semin Fetal Neonatal Med 2017;23:17: Values-based shared decision-making; physician vs parent definitions of good/bad outcomes.
Nakagawa TA et al. Pediatrics 2011;128:e720 (reaffirmed 2015): Brain death criteria — >=37+0 wks; 2 exams >=24 h; no criteria for preterm.
Partridge JC, Wall SN. Pediatrics 1997;99:76: Opioid analgesia in W&W — double effect defined.
Truog RD et al. NEJM 2000;342:508: Neuromuscular blockers at withdrawal — ethically inappropriate.
Caeymaex L et al. Arch Dis Child FNE 2013;98:F26: Parental participation in EOL decisions -> less decisional regret, better long-term mental health.
Sigurdson K et al. Pediatrics 2019;144(2): Racial/ethnic NICU disparities — systematic review.
Freedman B. NEJM 1987;317:141: Clinical equipoise — genuine uncertainty = moral requirement for RCT.
Rushton CH et al. Am J Nurs 2016;116:40: Moral distress as catalyst for building moral resilience.
12. Self-Check Questions พร้อมเฉลย
Q: ระบุ 4 principles และยกตัวอย่าง NICU scenario ที่ principles ขัดแย้งกัน

ตอบ: (1) Autonomy/Parental authority, (2) Beneficence, (3) Nonmaleficence, (4) Justice. ตัวอย่าง: parents ต้องการ aggressive care ใน 23-week infant ที่ทีมประเมิน benefit น้อยมาก — parental authority vs beneficence/nonmaleficence. Principalism ไม่ช่วยเมื่อ principles ขัดกัน — ต้องใช้ shared decision making + ethics consultation.
Q: Trial of therapy approach ข้อดีคืออะไร? ต้องการอะไรในการทำให้สำเร็จ?

ตอบ: ข้อดี: individualized; honors parental values; avoids self-fulfilling prophecy; honest about uncertainty; allows re-evaluation. ต้องการ: strong ongoing clinician-parent relationship; NIH Outcomes Estimator + Fig 4.2 framework; systematic re-evaluation points; institutional guidelines as background.
Q: Withholding vs Withdrawing — ethics และ language สำคัญอย่างไร?

ตอบ: Ethics: identical moral weight (Wall & Partridge 1997). Emotion: withdrawal feels much harder — parents รู้สึกว่าสั่ง "kill" ลูก. ห้ามใช้ "withdraw support/care"; ใช้ "withdrawal of LSMT" / "redirection of care". 3 criteria: (1) inevitability of death, (2) ineffective treatment, (3) poor quality of life.
Q: Brain death criteria — ใช้กับใครได้? Pitfalls 2 ข้อ?

ตอบ: >=37+0 wks GA เท่านั้น; known cause; 2 exams >=24 h (>=12 h if >30 days). Pitfall 1: Preterm = NO criteria (developmental immaturity). Pitfall 2: Anencephaly ≠ brain death (brainstem function retained; not de facto organ donation candidate). Pitfall 3: apnea test -> bradycardia risk; consult hospital policy first.
Q: Double effect — opioids vs NMB ต่างกันอย่างไร? Groningen protocol คืออะไร?

ตอบ: Opioids: intent = relieve suffering; side effect = respiratory depression -> double effect -> ethically appropriate (Partridge 1997). NMB: intent = ensure death -> ethically inappropriate (Truog NEJM 2000). Groningen (Netherlands): deliberate ending of life in intractable neonatal suffering — most international authorities: unsupportable; should be abandoned.
Q: HHS research consent — เมื่อไหร่ต้องการ both parents?

ตอบ: Both parents required: (1) >minimal risk WITHOUT direct benefit (knowledge only); (2) special category. 1 parent sufficient: (1) <=minimal risk; (2) >minimal risk WITH direct benefit. Exception: one parent deceased, unknown, incompetent, not reasonably available, or sole legal guardian. SUPPORT trial: 23 IRBs approved; controversy -> renewed focus on consent optimization.
Q: ouRHOPE mnemonic คืออะไร? ใช้เมื่อไหร่?

ตอบ: ouRHOPE (Racine et al. Dev Med Child Neurol 2017;59:125): สำหรับ neurologic injury consultation. Reflection, Humility, Open-mindedness, Partnership, Engagement. ช่วย acknowledge differences in perceptions of disability ก่อน family meeting.
Q: ทำไม cultural sensitivity จึงสำคัญ? ยกตัวอย่าง 2 ข้อในบริบทไทย

ตอบ: Cultural sensitivity: ลด maternal morbidity และ neonatal outcome disparities. Thailand 1 — Buddhism: ผู้ป่วยสุดท้ายควรได้ฟัง Dharma; การถอด ventilator อาจขัดกับ merit-making beliefs — ให้ chaplain/monk เข้าพบ. Thailand 2 — Decision hierarchy: ปู่ย่า/สามีอาจเป็น primary decision maker — navigate respectfully; ensure mother is informed and empowered; screen for coercive relationships.

Day 102 Preview — Fanaroff Ch.5
บทต่อไป: Chapter 5 — Improving the Quality, Safety, and Equity of Neonatal Intensive Care (Fanaroff 12th ed.). เตรียมตัว: PDSA cycle, IHI Model for Improvement, SPC charts, HRO principles, Just Culture, VON metrics, FICare RCT, racial disparity in NICU outcomes — Ethics principles วันนี้ (justice → health equity) เชื่อมตรงกับ Day 102 QI content.
